steps.title: Compare old belief with findings
steps.content: Researchers had thought defensive ultrasound was limited to tiger moths, hawkmoths, and one geometer species. Keller and colleagues then tested moths from 252 genera across most families of large-bodied moths and found defensive clicks in 52 genera, including several geometer genera.

steps.title: Remove unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice A claims most moth genera lack bat defenses, but the study only reports which tested genera produced ultrasonic clicks. Choice B invents uses for ultrasound beyond avoiding predators. Choice C invents other anti-bat strategies. Choice D states that anti-bat ultrasound may be more common among large-bodied moths than researchers previously knew. The answer is D.
